questions.question: The Constituent Assembly of India was set up under the:
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly of India was set up under the Cabinet Mission Plan (1946). It first met on 9 December 1946 with Dr. Rajendra Prasad as its permanent chairman from 11 December 1946.

questions.question: Who was the first President of Indian Republic?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of the Republic of India (1950-1962), serving two terms. He was also the President of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.question: The Brihadeeswara Temple at Thanjavur was built in the year:
questions.explanation: The Brihadeeswara Temple (also called Raja Raja Peruvudaiyar Kovil or Tanjore Big Temple) was completed c. 1010 CE during the reign of Rajaraja I. The 66m (216 ft) vimana is one of the tallest in South India.

questions.question: The Constituent Assembly of India was set up based on the recommendations of:
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Mission Plan (1946, by Pethick-Lawrence, Stafford Cripps, A.V. Alexander) created the Constituent Assembly. It had 389 members (296 from British India + 93 from Princely States). First meeting: 9 December 1946; Dr. Rajendra Prasad was president.

questions.question: Which dynasty built the Brihadeeswarar Temple in Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: The Brihadeeswarar Temple was built by the Chola dynasty in the 11th century. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Chola dynasty?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya was the founder of the Chola dynasty. He ruled in the 9th century.

questions.question: What was the capital of the Pandya kingdom?
questions.explanation: Madurai was the capital of the Pandya kingdom. It was an important cultural and trade center.

questions.question: Which Chola king built the Gangaikonda Cholapuram temple?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I built the Gangaikonda Cholapuram temple in the 11th century. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Who was the most famous Pandya king?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya was the most famous Pandya king. He ruled in the 13th century.

questions.question: What was the name of the Chola-Pandya war that took place in the 13th century?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Singalanthakka was a war between the Cholas and Pandyas. It marked the decline of the Chola dynasty.

questions.question: Which of the following was a notable feature of the Chola administration?
questions.explanation: The Chola administration was known for its centralized bureaucracy. It had a well-organized system of governance.

questions.question: Who was the Chola king that conquered Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I conquered Sri Lanka in the 11th century. He was a powerful and expansionist king.

questions.question: What was the name of the Pandya king who built the Meenakshi Amman Temple?
questions.explanation: Kulasekara Pandya built the Meenakshi Amman Temple in Madurai. It is a famous Hindu temple.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major trade center of the Chola kingdom?
questions.explanation: Nagapattinam was a major trade center of the Chola kingdom. It was an important port city.

questions.question: What was the name of the Chola-Pandya war?
questions.explanation: The Vellar war was a series of conflicts between the Chola and Pandya kingdoms. It lasted for several centuries.

questions.question: What was the name of the Pandya king who defeated the Cholas?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Sundara Pandya defeated the Cholas in the 13th century. He was a powerful Pandya king.

questions.question: Which dynasty built the Meenakshi Amman Temple in Madurai?
questions.explanation: The Meenakshi Amman Temple was built by the Pandya dynasty. It is a famous Hindu temple in India.

questions.question: Who was the last Chola king?
questions.explanation: Rajendra III was the last Chola king. He ruled from 1246 to 1279 AD.

questions.question: What was the name of the Chola king who defeated the Pandyas?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I defeated the Pandyas and expanded the Chola empire. He ruled from 907 to 955 AD.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major port city of the Chola kingdom?
questions.explanation: Nagapattinam was a major port city of the Chola kingdom. It was an important center for trade and commerce.

questions.question: Who was the Chola king who built the Airavatesvara Temple?
questions.explanation: Rajendra II built the Airavatesvara Temple in 1160 AD. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: What was the name of the Pandya king who fought against the Cholas?
questions.explanation: Vira Pandya fought against the Cholas and tried to expand the Pandya kingdom. He ruled from 1251 to 1281 AD.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major achievement of the Chola dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Chola dynasty achieved all of the above, including development of Tamil literature, construction of temples, and expansion of trade. They were a major power in South India.

questions.question: Which dynasty ruled Tamil Nadu during the 9th to 13th centuries?
questions.explanation: The Chola dynasty ruled Tamil Nadu from the 9th to 13th centuries. They were known for their administrative and military prowess.

questions.question: What was the capital of the Chola kingdom?
questions.explanation: Tanjavur was the capital of the Chola kingdom during its peak. It was an important center of learning and culture.

questions.question: Who was the most famous Chola king?
questions.explanation: Raja Raja Chola was a renowned king of the Chola dynasty. He expanded the empire and built the Brihadeeswara temple.

questions.question: Which kingdom rivaled the Cholas in South India?
questions.explanation: The Pandyas were a powerful kingdom that rivaled the Cholas in South India. They ruled from Madurai and had a distinct culture.

questions.question: What was the name of the famous Chola temple?
questions.explanation: The Brihadeeswara temple was built by Raja Raja Chola. It is a UNESCO World Heritage site and a testament to Chola architecture.

questions.question: In which century did the Chola-Pandya conflict occur?
questions.explanation: The Chola-Pandya conflict occurred in the 10th century. It was a period of intense rivalry between the two kingdoms.

questions.question: Who built the Grand Anicut dam?
questions.explanation: Karikala Chola built the Grand Anicut dam. It is an ancient irrigation dam that still functions today.

questions.question: What was the name of the Chola naval expedition?
questions.explanation: The Chola naval expedition to Kadaram was a significant event. It marked the expansion of Chola power into Southeast Asia.

questions.question: Which Chola king conquered Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola conquered Sri Lanka. He expanded the Chola empire and established trade relations with neighboring kingdoms.

questions.question: What was the name of the Pandya king who resisted the Chola invasion?
questions.explanation: Vira Pandya resisted the Chola invasion. He was a brave king who fought against the Chola empire.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler is credited with the construction of the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I built the Brihadeeswarar Temple in Thanjavur, a masterpiece of Chola architecture.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom, known for its maritime trade, had its capital at which city?
questions.explanation: Madurai was the capital of the Pandya kingdom, famous for its trade with the Roman Empire.

questions.question: Which Chola king is known for his naval expedition to Kadaram (present-day Malaysia/Indonesia)?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I led a successful naval expedition to Kadaram, showcasing Chola maritime power.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the 'Maduraikanchi' and known for his patronage of Tamil literature was?
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan I, a Pandya king, is celebrated in the Sangam poem 'Maduraikanchi' for his patronage.

questions.question: The Chola empire reached its zenith under which ruler, who also built the new capital at Gangaikonda Cholapuram?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I built Gangaikonda Cholapuram and expanded the Chola empire to its peak.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler revived the kingdom's power in the 6th century CE after the Kalabhra interregnum?
questions.explanation: Kadungon is credited with reviving the Pandya dynasty and ending the Kalabhra rule.

questions.question: The famous 'Thiruvalluvar' statue at Kanyakumari is associated with which ancient Tamil kingdom's cultural heritage?
questions.explanation: Thiruvalluvar, author of the Thirukkural, is traditionally associated with the Pandya kingdom's patronage.

questions.question: Which Chola king defeated the Pandya king Maravarman Sundara Pandya and established Chola supremacy over the Pandya region?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I defeated the Pandya ruler and brought the Pandya territory under Chola control.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's decline in the 14th century was primarily due to invasions by which sultanate?
questions.explanation: The Delhi Sultanate under Malik Kafur invaded the Pandya kingdom, leading to its decline.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler is known for his administrative reforms, including the creation of the 'Valanadu' administrative division?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I implemented administrative reforms, dividing the empire into Valanadus for efficient governance.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Chola dynasty that captured Thanjavur in 850 CE?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya captured Thanjavur from the Muttaraiyar around 850 CE and founded the Chola dynasty.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler constructed the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I built the Brihadeeswarar Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage site, completed in 1010 CE.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is credited with expanding the Meenakshi Amman Temple complex?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandyan (13th century) was a prominent Pandya ruler who patronized the temple and Tamil literature.

questions.question: What title did Rajendra I assume after his victory over the Ganges region?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I assumed the title Gangaikonda Chola and founded the city Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom was a major exporter of which item to the Roman Empire?
questions.explanation: The Pandyas were famous for exporting pepper and other spices to the Roman Empire.

questions.question: Which early Chola king is mentioned in the Sangam poem 'Pattinappalai'?
questions.explanation: The Sangam poem Pattinappalai describes Karikala Chola's reign and his capital Puhar.

questions.question: The Battle of Venni, fought between the Cholas and the Cheras and Pandyas, is associated with which Chola king?
questions.explanation: Karikala Chola defeated the Cheras and Pandyas at the Battle of Venni, establishing Chola supremacy.

questions.question: Which local self-government institution in Chola administration referred to Brahmin villages?
questions.explanation: Sabhas were assemblies of Brahmin villages, while Ur and Nagaram were for non-Brahmins and trade centers.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is known for his victory over the Chera king Uthiyan Cheralathan?
questions.explanation: Nedum Chezhian, a Pandya king, defeated the Chera king Uthiyan Cheralathan as recorded in Sangam literature.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler built the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I, the greatest Chola king, constructed the Brihadeeswarar Temple (Peruvudaiyar Kovil) in Thanjavur around 1010 CE.

questions.question: The famous Pandyan king credited with patronizing the Tamil Sangam was:
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan, also known as Ariapatti, is celebrated in Sangam literature as a great patron of the Third Tamil Sangam.

questions.question: Which Chola king is known for his naval expedition to Kadaram (Srivijaya)?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I launched a successful naval campaign against the Srivijaya empire (Kadaram) in Southeast Asia around 1025 CE.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's capital during the Sangam age was:
questions.explanation: Madurai served as the primary capital of the Pandyan dynasty, known for its Tamil Sangam and Meenakshi Temple.

questions.question: The Chola emperor who assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Chola' after his conquest of the Ganges region was:
questions.explanation: Rajendra I defeated the Pala king and marched to the Ganges, thereafter calling himself Gangaikonda Chola and building the new capital Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: Which Pandyan ruler defeated the Cholas and established Pandya supremacy in the 13th century?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya (1251-1268 CE) revived Pandya power, defeating the Cholas and extending control up to the Godavari.

questions.question: The Chola administration was characterized by which autonomous village assembly?
questions.explanation: The 'Sabha' was the assembly of brahmins in Chola villages, managing local affairs through committees called 'Variyams'.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler built the famous 'Gangaikonda Cholapuram' temple?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I built the Gangaikonda Cholapuram temple and the new capital to commemorate his northern expedition.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the Tamil epic 'Silappadikaram' as the ruler who wrongly executed Kovalan is:
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan, the Pandya king, ordered the execution of Kovalan based on a false accusation, a key event in Silappadikaram.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler introduced the 'Kudavolai' system of election for village committees?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I is associated with the Kudavolai system, a method of drawing lots to select members for village administrative bodies.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with the successful naval expedition against the Srivijaya Empire in Southeast Asia?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I led a naval expedition to the Srivijaya Empire in the 11th century, asserting Chola dominance in Southeast Asia.

questions.question: Which Chola king built the Brihadeeswara Temple at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I constructed the Brihadeeswara Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage site, in the 11th century.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler led a naval expedition to Kadaram (Srivijaya)?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I's naval campaign in 1025 CE subdued the Srivijaya empire, expanding Chola influence in Southeast Asia.

questions.question: The ancient Greek work 'Periplus of the Erythraean Sea' mentions which Tamil kingdom for its pearl trade?
questions.explanation: The Periplus (1st century CE) refers to the Pandya kingdom, noting its pearl fisheries and trade with the Roman Empire.

questions.question: Which Chola king assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Chola'?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I adopted the title after his successful campaign to the Ganges, and built Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler expanded the kingdom to its greatest extent in the 13th century?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I (c. 1251–1268) conquered up to Nellore and Kannauj, marking the zenith of Pandya power.

questions.question: What was the smallest administrative unit in the Chola empire?
questions.explanation: The 'Ur' was a village assembly, the basic unit of local self-government under the Chola administration.

questions.question: Which port city of the Pandya kingdom was famous for its pearl fishery?
questions.explanation: Korkai, an ancient port on the Gulf of Mannar, was renowned for pearl diving and trade in the Sangam period.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler sent an embassy to the Chinese Song emperor in 1015 CE?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I dispatched a mission to the Song court, strengthening diplomatic and trade ties with China.

questions.question: Which Pandya king was defeated by Rajaraja Chola I, leading to the annexation of Pandya territory?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Rajasimha II was defeated by Rajaraja I around 990 CE, after which the region came under Chola control.

questions.question: The Chola king who completed the conquest of Sri Lanka and assumed the title 'Mummudi Chola' was:
questions.explanation: Rajendra I conquered Sri Lanka and also performed the 'Great Anointment' after his northern expedition, earning the title 'Mummudi Chola'.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is credited with patronizing the Tamil Sangam and is mentioned in the ancient Tamil epic 'Silappadikaram' is:
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan II, also known as 'Aariyap Padai Kadal Neduncheliyan', is the Pandyan king featured in Silappadikaram as the judge who wrongly executed Kovalan.

questions.question: Which Chola king founded the city of Gangaikonda Cholapuram and built the famous temple there?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I founded Gangaikonda Cholapuram to commemorate his victory over the Ganges, and constructed the magnificent Brihadeeswarar Temple there.

questions.question: At which port did the Pandya kingdom have extensive trade relations with the Roman Empire, as evidenced by archaeological finds?
questions.explanation: Korkai, the ancient port of the Pandyas, was a center for pearl trade and had extensive Roman contact, confirmed by coin hoards and artifacts.

questions.question: The famous 'Meenakshi Amman Temple' in Madurai was largely rebuilt during the rule of which Nayak ruler, who revived Pandya-era traditions?
questions.explanation: Thirumalai Nayak (1623–1659) constructed the main gopuram and mandapam of the Meenakshi Temple, blending Pandya architectural style with Vijayanagara influences.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler's inscriptions refer to the 'Brihadeeswarar Temple' as 'Dakshina Meru'?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I named the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur 'Dakshina Meru' (Southern Meru) and his inscriptions detail its construction and donations.

questions.question: The Pandya king Kadungon is historically significant for:
options: Defeating the Kalabhras and reviving Pandya rule
questions.explanation: Kadungon (c. 590–620 CE) overthrew Kalabhra rule and re-established the Pandya kingdom, marking the beginning of the 'First Pandyan Empire'.

questions.question: Which Chola king defeated the Pandya king Maravarman Sundara Pandya and later performed the coronation of his own nominee?
questions.explanation: Kulothunga I (1070–1120 CE) invaded the Pandya kingdom and placed his son as viceroy, effectively ending the early Pandya imperial line.

questions.question: The 'Uthiramerur inscriptions' of the Chola period are best known for their detailed description of:
questions.explanation: Uthiramerur inscriptions (c. 750–1000 CE) provide evidence of the Chola system of local administration, including ward committees and secret ballot for village assembly elections.

questions.question: The Pandya king who built the 'Vaigai Dam' and is mentioned in Kautilya's Arthashastra as a supplier of pearls to the Mauryan court is:
questions.explanation: Peroomakizh (also known as 'Palyagasalai Mudukudumi Peruvazhuthi') is referenced in the Arthashastra for pearl trade; he is traditionally credited with constructing the Vaigai Dam.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with the conquest of the Ganges region and the construction of the Gangaikonda Cholapuram temple?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I marched to the Ganges, assumed the title 'Gangaikonda', and built the Brihadeeswarar temple at Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is known for patronizing the Tamil Sangam and being mentioned in the epic 'Silappadikaram' is:
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan I (also called 'Netunjeliyan') is the Pandya king associated with the Sangam age and the story of Kannagi in Silappadikaram.

questions.question: What was the name of the village assembly system that functioned under the Cholas, known for its elaborate electoral process?
questions.explanation: The 'Sabha' was the assembly of brahmana villages (brahmadeya) that used the Kudavolai system of secret ballot for elections.

questions.question: The famous 'Anbil Plates' provide information about which Chola king's genealogy and his grant to a Jain scholar?
questions.explanation: The Anbil Plates of Sundara Chola (Parantaka II) record his genealogy and a grant to the Jain teacher Vajranandi.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler is credited with ending the Kalabhra interregnum and reviving Pandya power in the 6th century CE?
questions.explanation: Kadungon (c. 590–620 CE) defeated the Kalabhras and re-established Pandya rule, marking the beginning of the Pandya revival.

questions.question: The Chola administration's revenue term 'Kadmai' refers to:
questions.explanation: 'Kadmai' was the basic land revenue or tax on agricultural produce, typically one-sixth of the yield.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is associated with the construction of the Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple in Madurai?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Kulasekara I (c. 1268–1308) is credited with building the core of the Meenakshi Temple, though later expansions were by the Nayaks.

questions.question: The Chola king who defeated the Cheras at the battle of Venni and is mentioned in the 'Purananuru' is:
questions.explanation: Karikala Chola defeated the Cheras and Pandya allies at Venni, and his exploits are celebrated in Sangam literature like Purananuru.

questions.question: The Pandya port of 'Korkai' was famous for which trade commodity?
questions.explanation: Korkai, the ancient Pandya capital, was renowned for pearl fishing and trade, mentioned in Greek and Roman accounts.

questions.question: The Uttaramerur inscriptions, detailing village self-government, are associated with which Chola king?
questions.explanation: Parantaka Chola I issued the Uttaramerur inscriptions describing the sabha system.

questions.question: Which Chola king led a successful naval expedition to Kadaram (Sri Vijaya empire)?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I conducted a naval campaign against Sri Vijaya in 1025 CE.

questions.question: The Chola kingdom's emblem was the tiger. Which of the following was the emblem of the Pandya kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Pandya dynasty's emblem was the fish (mirasu).

questions.question: Which Pandya king is credited with reviving the Pandya dynasty after the Kalabhra interregnum?
questions.explanation: Kadungon overthrew the Kalabhras and re-established Pandya rule in the 6th century.

questions.question: The Velvikkudi copper plates, which shed light on early Pandya history, belong to which ruler?
questions.explanation: The Velvikkudi copper plates were issued by Pandya king Nedunjeliyan in the 8th century.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is traditionally associated with the original construction of the Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple?
questions.explanation: Kulashekara Pandyan is credited with building the Meenakshi temple in Madurai.

questions.question: The Pandya king defeated by the Pallava king Narasimhavarman I at the battle of Manimangalam was:
questions.explanation: Narasimhavarman I defeated the Pandya king Sendhan (Maravarman Avanichulam) around 642 CE.

questions.question: Which Chola king assumed the title 'Mummudi Chola' (three-crowned Chola) after his conquests?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I assumed 'Mummudi Chola' after conquering the Chera and Pandya kingdoms.

questions.question: The ancient port of Korkai was the capital of which early kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Pandya kingdom had its early capital at Korkai before moving to Madurai.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with the construction of the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I built the Brihadeeswarar Temple in the 11th century, a masterpiece of Chola architecture.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler introduced the 'Kudavolai' (pot ticket) system for village administration?
questions.explanation: The Kudavolai system, an electoral method for village officers, is recorded in Uttiramerur inscriptions of Parantaka I.

questions.question: The ancient Tamil Sangam flourished under the patronage of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Pandya kings were renowned patrons of the Tamil Sangam, the literary academies of ancient Tamilakam.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Cholan' after marching to the Ganges?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I earned the title after his successful expedition to the Ganges, later founding Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: The original capital of the Pandya kingdom was Korkai; it was later moved to which city?
questions.explanation: The early Pandya capital was Korkai, but it was later shifted to Madurai, which became their cultural and political center.

questions.question: Which Chola king is known for launching naval expeditions to Southeast Asia and expanding Chola influence?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I's naval campaigns reached Srivijaya, establishing Chola dominance in Southeast Asian waters.

questions.question: The Uttiramerur inscriptions of the Chola period provide detailed information about which aspect of administration?
questions.explanation: The Uttiramerur inscriptions describe the Kudavolai system and the functioning of village assemblies under Parantaka I.

questions.question: Which animal was the royal emblem of the Chola dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Chola emblem was the tiger (Puli), while the Pandya used the fish and Chera used the bow.

questions.question: Which ancient South Indian kingdom was renowned for its pearl fisheries, mentioned in Greek and Roman texts?
questions.explanation: The Pandya coast was famous for pearl fishing, as recorded in the Periplus of the Erythraean Sea and other sources.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler is credited with defeating the Kalabhras and re-establishing Pandya rule in the 6th century CE?
questions.explanation: Kadungon (Kadunkon) ended the Kalabhra interregnum and revived the Pandya dynasty, as per early Pandya inscriptions.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is known for patronizing the Tamil Sangam and is mentioned in the epic Silappadikaram?
questions.explanation: Nedunchezhiyan appears in Silappadikaram as the judge who ordered Kovalan's execution.

questions.question: The Chola expedition to North India and the establishment of the new capital Gangaikonda Cholapuram is associated with which ruler?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I marched to the Ganges and founded Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: What was the title assumed by the Pandya king Maravarman Sundara Pandya I after his victory over the Hoysalas?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Sundara Pandya I assumed the title Tribhuvana Chakravarti meaning Emperor of the Three Worlds.

questions.question: Which Chola king was known by the name 'Uttama Chola' and issued inscriptions regarding land grants?
questions.explanation: Uttama Chola reigned from 973 to 985 CE and his name itself was the title.

questions.question: The famous Pandya port of Korkai was located on which river?
questions.explanation: Korkai, the early Pandya capital, was situated on the Tamiraparani river.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler issued the famous 'Larger Leiden Grant' and 'Smaller Leiden Grant'?
