questions.question: What was the emblem of the Chola dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Chola emblem was the tiger; the Pandya emblem was the fish and Chera the bow.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is credited with building the Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple in Madurai?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I built the Meenakshi temple complex in the 13th century.

questions.question: Which type of local assembly was prevalent in Brahmin villages (Brahmadeya) under the Cholas?
questions.explanation: Sabha was the assembly for Brahmin villages; Ur for non-Brahmin villages; Nagaram for traders.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with the successful naval expedition against the Srivijaya Empire?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I led a naval expedition in 1025 CE against the Srivijaya Empire in Southeast Asia.

questions.question: The Pandya king who defeated the Chola king Rajaraja II at the Battle of Nettur is:
questions.explanation: Maravarman Sundara Pandya I defeated Rajaraja II at the Battle of Nettur, marking a Pandya resurgence.

questions.question: The famous 'Larger Leyden Grant' inscription belongs to which Chola king?
questions.explanation: The Larger Leyden Grant is a copper-plate inscription of Rajaraja I detailing grants to a Buddhist monastery.

questions.question: The Chola administrative unit 'Valanadu' was divided into which smaller units?
questions.explanation: A Valanadu was subdivided into Kurrams or Nadus, which were further divided into villages (Ur).

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the ancient Tamil Sangam literature as the patron of the poet Mangudi Marudanar is:
questions.explanation: Mudukudumi Peruvazhuthi is praised in the Purananuru for his generosity and patronage of Sangam poets.

questions.question: Which Chola king assumed the title 'Mummudi Chola'?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I took the title Mummudi Chola, meaning 'Chola with three crowns' (Chola, Pandya, Chera).

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom was finally annexed by the British East India Company after which event?
questions.explanation: The Polygar Wars (1799-1805) led to the British annexation of the Pandya territories in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The Chola king who built the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur is:
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I constructed the Brihadeeswarar Temple (Peruvudaiyar Kovil) in Thanjavur around 1010 CE.

questions.question: The Pandya king who revived the kingdom after the Kalabhra interregnum was:
questions.explanation: Kadungon (c. 590–620 CE) ended the Kalabhra rule and re-established the Pandya dynasty in Madurai.

questions.question: Which Chola king built the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I (985–1014 CE) commissioned the Brihadeeswarar Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage site, in Thanjavur.

questions.question: Which of the following symbols was used on Pandya coins?
questions.explanation: The Pandya emblem was the fish (minaveli), often depicted on their coins and flag.

questions.question: The Chola invasion of Srivijaya (Kedah) was undertaken by which king?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I led a naval expedition in 1025 CE against Srivijaya to secure trade routes.

questions.question: The 'Kudavolai' system in Chola administration was associated with which aspect?
questions.explanation: Kudavolai was a pot-ticket method used for electing village committee members (ur) in Chola villages.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is known for defeating the Chera and Chola at the Battle of Talaiyalanganam?
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan, a Sangam-era Pandya ruler, won the Battle of Talaiyalanganam against a confederacy of enemies.

questions.question: The title 'Muvendavelan' in Chola administration refers to?
options: Officials serving the three crowned kings
questions.explanation: Muvendavelan denoted Brahmin officials appointed by the Chola, Pandya, and Chera kings for inter-kingdom service.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler issued the famous Uttiramerur inscriptions on local self-government?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I (907–955 CE) issued the Uttiramerur inscriptions detailing village assembly elections and functions.

questions.question: The early capital of the Pandya kingdom was?
questions.explanation: Korkai served as the first Pandya capital before shifting to Madurai due to trade and strategic reasons.

questions.question: Which Chola king assumed the title 'Gangai Kondan' after his expedition to the Ganges?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I earned the title 'Gangai Kondan' (the one who conquered the Ganges) after his north Indian campaign.

questions.question: The Chola king who defeated the Pandya ruler Maravarman Sundara Pandya I and established Chola supremacy in the Pandya country was?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I defeated the Pandya ruler and annexed the Pandya kingdom, bringing it under Chola control.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is credited with reviving the Pandya power after the decline of the Cholas and patronizing the Tamil Sangam is?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I (1251-1268 CE) revived Pandya power and is known for his patronage of Tamil literature.

questions.question: The famous Chola bronze sculpture of 'Nataraja' was primarily patronized during the reign of which Chola queen?
questions.explanation: Sembiyan Mahadevi, a Chola queen, was a major patron of bronze sculptures, including the Nataraja icon.

questions.question: Which Pandya port city was a major center of trade with the Roman Empire, known for its pearl fisheries?
questions.explanation: Korkai, the ancient Pandya port, was famous for pearl fisheries and trade with Rome.

questions.question: The Chola king who assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Chola' after his successful northern expedition to the Ganges was?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Chola' after his march to the Ganges and founded the new capital Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: The Pandya king who defeated the Chola king Rajaraja II and later faced the invasion of the Delhi Sultanate under Malik Kafur was?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Kulasekara Pandya I (1268-1308 CE) defeated the Cholas but his kingdom was later invaded by Malik Kafur.

questions.question: The Chola administration was known for its efficient local self-government, especially the 'Kudavolai' system, which was a method of?
questions.explanation: The Kudavolai system was a unique method of electing members to the village assembly (ur) by drawing lots.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is credited with building the famous Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple in Madurai?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Sundara Pandya I (1216-1238 CE) is traditionally credited with the initial construction of the Meenakshi Temple.

questions.question: The Chola king who fought the Battle of Takkolam (949 CE) against the Rashtrakutas and was killed in the battle was?
questions.explanation: Rajaditya, the Chola prince, was killed in the Battle of Takkolam while fighting the Rashtrakuta king Krishna III.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's capital was shifted from Korkai to Madurai during the reign of which early Pandya king?
questions.explanation: Kadungon (590-620 CE) revived the Pandya dynasty and shifted the capital from Korkai to Madurai.

questions.question: Who is considered the founder of the medieval Chola dynasty (c. 850 CE)?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya captured Thanjavur from the Muttaraiyars in c. 850 CE and established the medieval Chola line.

questions.question: Which city served as the capital of the Pandya kingdom during the Sangam age?
questions.explanation: Madurai was the capital of the early Pandyas and is often referred to as the 'city of four temples' and a center of Tamil Sangam literature.

questions.question: The Chola administrative division known as 'Valanadu' was a group of which smaller units?
questions.explanation: Nadus were grouped into Valanadus under the Chola administration, with Valanadu being an intermediate unit between Nadu and Mandalam.

questions.question: The famous Chola bronze statue of Nataraja (Lord Shiva as Cosmic Dancer) is a masterpiece from which period?
questions.explanation: The Nataraja bronze icon reached its zenith under Chola patronage, especially during the 10th–12th centuries.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler is credited with patronizing the third Tamil Sangam at Madurai?
questions.explanation: Nedunchezhiyan I, also known as 'Pal Yagasalai' Mudukudumi Peruvazhuti's descendant, is traditionally associated with the third Sangam.

questions.question: The Chola navy's successful overseas expedition against the Srivijaya kingdom was led by which ruler?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I (1012–1044 CE) launched a naval campaign against Srivijaya (Kadaram) around 1025 CE to assert Chola supremacy in Southeast Asia.

questions.question: Which land revenue term in the Chola administration referred to tax collected in kind (grain)?
questions.explanation: Kadamai was a land tax usually paid in paddy or grain, while Vari was an umbrella term for taxes, and Kudimai referred to forced labor.

questions.question: The Kallanai (Grand Anicut) across the river Kaveri is attributed to which early Chola ruler?
questions.explanation: Karikala Chola (c. 2nd century CE) is traditionally credited with building the Kallanai, an ancient masonry dam, to divert water for irrigation.

questions.question: Which Pandya king defeated the Chola ruler Rajaraja II and revived Pandya power in the 13th century?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Sundara Pandya (c. 1216–1238 CE) defeated Rajaraja II and expanded Pandya territory after a period of Chola dominance.

questions.question: The Pandyan ruler who defeated the Cholas at the Battle of Takkolam and revived Pandya power in the 9th century was:
questions.explanation: Varagunavarman II (c. 862-880 CE) defeated the Cholas at Takkolam and restored Pandya hegemony.

questions.question: Which Chola emperor's naval expedition led to the conquest of Srivijaya and parts of Southeast Asia?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I's naval campaign (c. 1025 CE) against the Srivijaya empire extended Chola influence across Southeast Asia.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's emblem was the:
questions.explanation: The Pandya emblem was a pair of fishes, while the Chola emblem was a tiger and the Chera emblem a bow.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler bore the title 'Gangaikonda Chola' after his conquest of the Ganges region?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Chola' after his successful expedition to the Ganges river basin.

questions.question: The Pandyan king who is known for patronizing the Tamil literary work 'Tirukkural' (though traditionally associated with the Sangam age) and also built the Meenakshi Amman Temple's early structure was:
questions.explanation: Nedumaran (also known as Koon Pandyan) is credited with patronizing Tamil literature and constructing the original Meenakshi temple.

questions.question: The Chola administrative system featured which of the following local self-government units?
questions.explanation: Chola administration had units like Nadu, Kurram, and Valanadu, along with the famous village assemblies called 'ur' or 'sabha'.

questions.question: Which Pandyan ruler was defeated by the Chola king Parantaka I at the Battle of Vellore (or Tellaru) around 910 CE?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I defeated Varagunavarman II at Vellore (or Tellaru), leading to the temporary decline of the Pandya kingdom.

questions.question: The Chola period bronze statue of 'Nataraja' (Shiva as the cosmic dancer) is considered a masterpiece of which metalwork technique?
questions.explanation: Chola bronzes, including the Nataraja, were created using the lost-wax (cire perdue) casting technique, renowned for its detail.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's capital during the Sangam age and later periods was:
questions.explanation: Madurai served as the Pandya capital from the Sangam era through the medieval period, with Korkai being an earlier port capital.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler built the Brihadeeswara temple at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I constructed the Brihadeeswara Temple in 1010 CE.

questions.question: The ancient Pandya capital during the Sangam period was?
questions.explanation: Madurai was the capital of the Pandya kingdom.

questions.question: Which Chola king assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Cholan' after his expedition to the Ganges?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I led a successful campaign to the Ganges.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's emblem (flag symbol) was the?
questions.explanation: The Pandya emblem was the fish (matsya).

questions.question: The Chola king who conquered Madurai and took the title 'Maduraikonda' was?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I defeated the Pandyas and captured Madurai.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major trade partner of the Pandya kingdom during the Roman era?
questions.explanation: The Pandyas had extensive trade with the Roman Empire.

questions.question: The famous 'Nataraja' bronze sculpture is a masterpiece of which dynasty's art?
questions.explanation: Chola bronze casting reached its zenith with the Nataraja figure.

questions.question: The 'Sabha' in Chola administration referred to the assembly of which group?
questions.explanation: The Sabha was the assembly of Brahmin villages.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is known for the construction of the Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple complex?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandyan (13th century) expanded the Meenakshi temple.

questions.question: The 'Uraiyur' town was the early capital of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Uraiyur was the ancient capital of the Cholas.

questions.question: The Chola king who successfully invaded the Ganga valley and brought the holy water of the Ganges to the Chola country is:
questions.explanation: Rajendra I (1012-1044 CE) undertook a northern expedition, defeated Mahipala of Bengal, and brought Ganges water to establish a new capital called Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is credited with demolishing the Madurai Meenakshi Amman Temple and then rebuilding it with a new gopuram is:
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I (1251-1268 CE) is known for his patronage of the Meenakshi Temple; the existing structure largely dates from his period.

questions.question: The practice of 'Kudavolai' system of electing village representatives was associated with which Chola administrative unit?
questions.explanation: The 'Ur' was the primary village assembly in the Chola period, and the Kudavolai system involved drawing lots to select members for various committees.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler assumed the title 'Raja Kesari Varman' and issued the famous Leyden copper plate grant?
questions.explanation: Kulottunga I (1070-1122 CE) used the title Raja Kesari Varman and granted the Leyden plates to the Buddhist monastery at Negapatam.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom reached its zenith of power under which ruler, who also defeated the Hoysala king Somesvara?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I (1251-1268 CE) extended Pandya power and defeated the Hoysala king Somesvara at Kannanur.

questions.question: The temple of Brihadeeswara at Thanjavur was built by Rajaraja I. Which architect is traditionally believed to have built it?
questions.explanation: Kunjaramallan, a chief architect of Rajaraja I, is credited with the construction of the Brihadeeswara Temple.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is known to have sent an embassy to the Roman Emperor Augustus is:
questions.explanation: According to the Roman historian Strabo, an embassy from the Pandya king (identified as Nedunchezhiyan I) visited Augustus around 20 BCE.

questions.question: The Chola ruler who introduced the practice of inscribing his conquests on the walls of the Tanjore temple is:
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I (985-1014 CE) had detailed inscriptions of his military campaigns recorded on the walls of the Brihadeeswara Temple.

questions.question: Which Pandya capital was sacked by the Chola king Parantaka I, leading to a long conflict between the two dynasties?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I of the Chola dynasty captured Madurai, the Pandya capital, around 920 CE, sparking a prolonged struggle.

questions.question: The famous literary work 'Tolkappiyam' belongs to which period, often associated with the Pandya patronage?
questions.explanation: Tolkappiyam, the earliest Tamil grammar, is dated to the Sangam age (c. 300 BCE - 300 CE), when Pandya kings were prominent patrons of Tamil literature.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler founded the city of Gangaikonda Cholapuram?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I built Gangaikonda Cholapuram after his successful campaign to the Ganges river.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's emblematic fish (meen) is associated with which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Pandya emblem featured a pair of fish, symbolizing their maritime and riverine dominance.

questions.question: The famous port of Korkai was associated with which ancient Tamil kingdom?
questions.explanation: Korkai was the chief port of the Pandya kingdom, known for pearl fishing and trade with Rome.

questions.question: Which Chola king defeated the Pandya ruler and assumed the title 'Maduranthaka'?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I defeated the Pandyas and captured Madurai, earning the title 'Maduranthaka' (destroyer of Madurai).

questions.question: The Pandya ruler who is credited with sending an embassy to the Roman Emperor Augustus is:
questions.explanation: Nedunchezhiyan I (also called Pandya of the Sangam age) sent an embassy to Augustus around 26 BCE.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler is known for his naval expedition to Kadaram (Sri Lanka/Sumatra)?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I led a naval campaign to Kadaram (Srivijaya empire) in the 11th century, expanding Chola influence across Southeast Asia.

questions.question: The title 'Pandya Nadu' was adopted by which Chola king after subjugating the Pandyas?
questions.explanation: Kulothunga Chola I reasserted Chola control over Pandya territory and used the title 'Pandya Nadu' after subduing revolts.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler fought against the Chola king Raja Raja I at the battle of Velur?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Rajasimha II was the Pandya ruler defeated by Raja Raja I at the battle of Velur (or Vellore).

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's capital Madurai was associated with which literary academy?
questions.explanation: Madurai hosted the Sangam academies, ancient Tamil literary assemblies patronized by the Pandya kings.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is credited with patronizing the Tamil Sangam and known as 'Valuthi'?
questions.explanation: Mudukudumi Peruvaluthi is a famous Pandya king mentioned in Sangam literature.

questions.question: Which Chola king assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Chola' after his victory over the Gangas?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I assumed the title after his northern expedition and built Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's capital was initially at:
questions.explanation: The early Pandya capital was Korkai, a port city, later moved to Madurai.

questions.question: Which Chola king defeated the Pandya king Rajasimha II and annexed the Pandya kingdom?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I defeated Rajasimha II and brought Pandya territory under Chola control.

questions.question: The famous 'Thiruvilayadal Puranam' is associated with which Pandya king?
questions.explanation: The legend of 'Thiruvilayadal' (divine sports) is linked to the Pandya king Nedunchezhiyan.

questions.question: Which Chola king is known for his naval expedition to Kadaram (Malay Peninsula)?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I led a naval expedition to Kadaram (Srivijaya empire) in the 11th century.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is known for his victory at the Battle of Tondaimandalam against the Pallavas?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Rajasimha I (8th century) defeated the Pallavas and expanded Pandya territory.

questions.question: The Chola king who introduced the system of 'Kudavolai' (lottery) for village administration?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I (907-955 CE) is associated with the Kudavolai system of village elections.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler was a contemporary of the Chola king Rajaraja I and was defeated by him?
questions.explanation: Rajasimha II was the Pandya king defeated by Rajaraja I around 990 CE.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with building the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I completed the Brihadeeswarar Temple in 1010 AD, a masterpiece of Chola architecture.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom at its peak had active maritime trade with which ancient empire?
questions.explanation: The Pandyas traded spices and pearls with the Roman Empire, as evidenced by Roman coins found in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Chola' after his northern campaign?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I defeated the rulers of the Ganges region and founded the city Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.question: The Meenakshi Temple at Madurai was originally built under the patronage of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple was originally built by the Pandya king Kulashekhara Pandya.

questions.question: The ancient Tamil epic 'Silappadikaram' mentions the judgment of which Pandya king?
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan is the Pandya king who wrongfully executed Kovalan, leading to Kannagi's curse.

questions.question: Under the Chola administrative system, what was the smallest unit of administration?
questions.explanation: The 'Ur' was the village assembly, the smallest unit, while 'Nadu' and 'Valanadu' were larger divisions.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is traditionally associated with founding the Tamil Sangam?
questions.explanation: Ugraperuvazhuthi (Ukkira Peruvaluthi) is credited with establishing the first Tamil Sangam according to tradition.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Chola-Pandya conflict in the 10th century:
1. Parantaka I defeated the Pandya king Rajasimha II and assumed the title 'Maduraikonda'.
2. The Pandya king Rajasimha II sought help from the Sinhalese king Mahinda IV.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I defeated Rajasimha II at Vellur and took the title Maduraikonda; Rajasimha II fled to Ceylon and allied with Mahinda IV, making both statements correct.

questions.question: The 'Kudavolai' system of election to the village assembly during the Chola period is best described as:
options: Direct election by all adult male residents
Selection by the council of ministers
questions.explanation: Kudavolai involved writing candidates' names on palm-leaf slips and drawing them from a pot, a form of lottery election.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is credited with the construction of the Meenakshi Temple in Madurai?
questions.explanation: The current Meenakshi Temple structure, including its famous gopurams, was largely built by Jatavarman Kulasekara Pandya in the 13th century.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the Chola administration:
1. Valangai and Idangai were social divisions representing the right-hand and left-hand castes.
2. The 'Sabha' was the assembly of the brahmana villages (Brahmadeya).
3. The 'Ur' was the assembly of non-brahmana villages.
Which of the statements are correct?
questions.explanation: All three are accurate: Valangai/Idangai were caste divisions, Sabha governed Brahmadeya villages, and Ur governed ordinary villages.

questions.question: The Chola king Rajaraja I is known for which of the following administrative reforms?
options: Conducting a land survey and standardizing measurements
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I carried out a comprehensive land survey and standardized measurements, famously known as the 'Ulagalantha Perumal' survey.

questions.question: The term 'Mummudi Chola' appearing in Chola inscriptions indicates:
options: The king who conquered three regions (Chola, Pandya, Chera)
The king who wore three crowns
The king who performed three Vedic sacrifices
The king who ruled for three decades
questions.explanation: 'Mummudi' literally means three crowns, symbolizing the Chola king's sovereignty over the Chola, Pandya, and Chera kingdoms.

questions.question: Which of the following coins were issued by the Pandya kings?
options: Square copper coins with elephant symbol
Copper coins with bow and arrow
questions.explanation: The Pandya dynasty's emblem was the fish, and their gold coins commonly bore this fish design.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the Chola navy:
1. Rajendra Chola's naval expedition to Kadaram (Sri Vijaya) was aimed at dominating the Southeast Asian trade routes.
2. The Chola navy used a type of ship called 'Kolandi' (a coastal vessel).
Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Rajendra's Kadaram expedition targeted trade dominance, and the Kolandi ship type is mentioned in Chola inscriptions, making both statements correct.

questions.question: The Pandya king 'Kadungon' is credited with:
options: Defeating the Kalabhras and reviving Pandya rule
Sending an embassy to the Roman Emperor
questions.explanation: Kadungon (6th century AD) overthrew the Kalabhra interregnum and re-established the Pandya kingdom.

questions.question: The 'Nadu' assembly during the Chola period was composed of:
options: Representatives of the villages within the Nadu
questions.explanation: The Nadu assembly consisted of representatives from the constituent villages of that district, handling local administration.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with the complete subjugation of the Pandya kingdom and the assumption of the title 'Maduraikonda'?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I defeated the Pandya king Rajasimha II and captured Madurai, assuming the title 'Maduraikonda' (Conqueror of Madurai).

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the 'Velvikudi Grant' as having performed the 'Hiranyagarbha' and 'Tulabhara' rituals to restore his kingdom's glory was:
questions.explanation: The Velvikudi Grant of the Pandya king Parantaka Nedunjadaiyan (Varaguna I) records his performance of these rituals to atone for his father's defeat.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler's 'Rajarajeswaram' temple inscription provides crucial evidence for the Chola-Pandya conflict, mentioning the defeat of the Pandya king 'Amarabhujanga'?
questions.explanation: The Brihadeeswarar temple (Rajarajeswaram) inscription of Rajaraja I lists the Pandya king Amarabhujanga among his defeated enemies.

questions.question: The Pandya king 'Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I' is historically significant for:
options: Defeating the Chola king Rajaraja III
Establishing the Pandya capital at Madurai
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I defeated the Chola king Rajaraja III at the battle of Tellaru, marking the resurgence of Pandya power.

questions.question: Which Chola king's 'Mysore' or 'Kannada' inscriptions refer to a Pandya rebellion that was crushed, leading to the execution of the Pandya king 'Vira Pandya'?
questions.explanation: Kulottunga I's inscriptions from the Mysore region record the suppression of a Pandya rebellion and the beheading of Vira Pandya.

questions.question: The 'Thirumalai' inscription of the Pandya king 'Jatavarman Kulasekhara I' is a key source for understanding:
options: The Chola-Pandya naval battle at Kandalur
The Hoysala interference in Pandya affairs
The establishment of the Pandya 'Tenkasi' branch
The construction of the Srirangam temple gopuram
questions.explanation: The Thirumalai inscription details the Pandya victory over the Hoysala king Ramanatha, highlighting Hoysala involvement in the Chola-Pandya succession wars.

questions.question: Which Chola king's reign saw the famous 'Pandya-Chola' war where the Pandya king 'Srimara Srivallabha' was defeated at the battle of 'Kumbakonam'?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I defeated the Pandya king Srimara Srivallabha at the battle of Kumbakonam (or Arisil) around 910 CE.

questions.question: The 'Maduraikkanchi' poem, part of the 'Pattuppattu' anthology, provides a vivid description of the capital of which Pandya king?
questions.explanation: The Maduraikkanchi describes the prosperity of Madurai under the rule of the Pandya king Nedunjeliyan (also known as 'Arya Padai Kadantha Nedunjeliyan').

questions.question: Which Chola king's 'Leyden Grant' mentions the defeat of the Pandya king 'Rajasimha II' and the subsequent establishment of Chola control over the Pandya country?
questions.explanation: The Leyden Grant of Aditya I records his victory over the Pandya king Rajasimha II, paving the way for Chola hegemony in the south.

questions.question: The 'Pandya' king who is credited with building the 'Kailasanathar Temple' at Kanchipuram, a site later associated with the Pallavas and Cholas, is:
questions.explanation: Varaguna II, a Pandya king, is credited with constructing the Kailasanathar temple at Kanchipuram, though the site is more famously linked to the Pallava Rajasimha.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Chola administration under Rajaraja I and Rajendra I: (i) Rajaraja I conducted a detailed land survey known as 'Kudavolai' system. (ii) Rajendra I assumed the title 'Gangaikonda Chola' after his northern campaign. (iii) The Chola navy under Rajendra I launched an expedition against the Srivijaya empire. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Kudavolai was a system of election, not a land survey; Rajendra I did take the title Gangaikonda Chola; his navy attacked Srivijaya (Kadaram). So (ii) and (iii) are correct.

questions.question: The Pandya kingdom's port of Korkai was a major center for which of the following trades during the early historic period?
options: Pearl fishing and trade with Rome
questions.explanation: Korkai was renowned for pearl fisheries and was a key port in the Indo-Roman trade, as mentioned in Sangam literature and Periplus.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the 'Sabha' in the Chola administrative system?
options: An assembly of the general village population
A committee of Brahmins managing temple endowments
An assembly of merchants in trade centers
A council of elders overseeing irrigation works
questions.explanation: The 'Sabha' was a Brahmin assembly (mahasabha) in brahmadeya villages, responsible for local administration and temple management.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is credited with patronizing the Third Tamil Sangam at Madurai and is mentioned in the epic 'Silappadikaram' is:
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan II (also known as 'Aariyap Padai Kadantha' Nedunjeliyan) is the Pandya king who presided over the Third Sangam and appears in Silappadikaram as the judge of Kannagi's story.

questions.question: The Chola bronze sculptures, such as the Nataraja, were typically cast using the 'lost-wax' process. Which of the following metals is NOT a component of the traditional Panchaloha alloy used for these icons?
questions.explanation: Panchaloha consists of gold, silver, copper, tin, and lead; zinc is not a traditional component in South Indian bronze casting.

questions.question: The Battle of Takkolam (c. 949 CE) was a decisive conflict between which two powers, leading to the temporary eclipse of the Chola power?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Takkolam was fought between the Chola king Parantaka I and the Rashtrakuta king Krishna III, resulting in a Chola defeat and loss of the northern territories.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler was defeated by Rajendra Chola I at the Battle of the River Vaippar, leading to the annexation of the Pandya kingdom into the Chola empire?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Rajasimha I was the Pandya king defeated by Rajendra Chola I around 1019 CE, after which the Pandya region became a Chola province.

questions.question: The Chola king Vijayalaya captured the city of Thanjavur from which ruling dynasty around 850 CE, marking the beginning of the Imperial Chola period?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya defeated the Muttaraiyar chieftains who were feudatories of the Pallavas, and established Thanjavur as his capital.

questions.question: The Meenakshi Amman Temple in Madurai, though expanded by the Nayaks, was originally built by the Pandyas. Which Pandya king is traditionally credited with its foundation?
questions.explanation: Kadungon (c. 6th century CE) is considered the founder of the later Pandya dynasty and is traditionally associated with the initial construction of the Meenakshi temple.

questions.question: In the Chola revenue system, the term 'Kadamai' referred to:
options: A tax on trade and commerce
A land tax paid in kind or cash
questions.explanation: Kadamai was the basic land tax, usually one-sixth of the produce, collected from peasants by the Chola state.

questions.question: The Kudavolai system of electing members to the village assembly is described in which Chola inscription?
questions.explanation: The Uttaramerur inscriptions of Parantaka Chola I describe the Kudavolai system of drawing lots to elect members.

questions.question: Which Pandya inscription mentions the dynasty's origin from the Moon and the first Tamil Sangam?
questions.explanation: The Sinnamanur copper plates of the Pandya king describe the lunar origin and patronage of the first Sangam.

questions.question: Which Chola king built the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur and what is the approximate height of its vimana?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I constructed the temple; its vimana is about 66 meters (216 ft) tall, a masterpiece of granite architecture.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler faced the invasion of Malik Kafur in 1311 CE and lost his kingdom?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Kulashekhara I, the last great Pandya king, was defeated by Malik Kafur's Delhi Sultanate forces.

questions.question: Which Chola king completed the conquest of the Pandya kingdom and assumed the title 'Pandya-kul-asani'?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I fully subjugated the Pandyas and used the title 'Pandya-kul-asani' (thunderbolt to the Pandya race).

questions.question: In Chola administration, which official was in charge of the royal treasury?
questions.explanation: Bhandagarika was the title for the treasurer responsible for the royal treasury in the Chola bureaucracy.

questions.question: The collection of Shaiva hymns known as 'Tirumurai' was organized during the reign of which Chola king?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja Chola I discovered the hymns of the Nayanmars and had them compiled as the Tirumurai.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of Chola king and his achievement is correctly matched?
options: Parantaka I – Conquest of Sri Lanka
Aditya I – Capture of Thanjavur from Mutharaiyar
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I indeed conquered Gangavadi (Western Ganga region); the other matches are historically incorrect.

questions.question: Which Chola king is famous for leading a naval expedition against the Srivijaya Empire?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I launched a successful naval campaign against Srivijaya in the 11th century to assert dominance.

questions.question: In the Chola revenue system, what did the term 'Valanadu' refer to?
questions.explanation: Valanadu was a large administrative division within a Chola mandalam, used for revenue management.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the 'Mandagapattu inscription' of the Pallava king Mahendravarman I as having been defeated, belongs to which early Pandya lineage?
questions.explanation: Kadungon is credited with reviving the Pandya dynasty after the Kalabhra interregnum and was defeated by Mahendravarman I as per the Mandagapattu inscription.

questions.question: The famous 'Velvikudi Grant' of the Pandya king Nedunjadaiyan (Varaguna I) primarily records which significant event?
options: Victory over the Pallavas at Tellaru
questions.explanation: The Velvikudi Grant records the Pandya victory over the Pallava king Nandivarman II at the battle of Tellaru, restoring Pandya prestige.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler's reign saw the famous 'Kalingattu Parani' composed, and what does this text primarily celebrate?
options: Rajendra I; his naval expedition to Kadaram
Kulothunga I; his victory in the Kalinga war
Rajaraja I; the construction of the Brihadeeswarar Temple
Vikrama Chola; his patronage of Tamil literature
questions.explanation: Kulothunga I's general Karunakara Tondaiman led the successful Kalinga campaign, which is celebrated in the epic poem 'Kalingattu Parani' by Jayamkondar.

questions.question: The Pandya king Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I is known for his extensive military campaigns. Which of the following regions did he NOT successfully invade?
questions.explanation: While Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I conquered the Hoysalas, the Kongu region, and Ceylon, the Chola naval expedition to Kadaram was under Rajendra I, not a Pandya campaign.

questions.question: The 'Uttaramerur Inscriptions' of the Chola period are most significant for providing detailed information on which aspect of Chola administration?
questions.explanation: The Uttaramerur inscriptions, from the reign of Parantaka I, provide a detailed constitution for the functioning of the village assembly (Sabha), including qualifications and election procedures.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is credited with commissioning the construction of the 'Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple' in Madurai in its current structural form?
questions.explanation: Maravarman Kulasekara Pandya I (1268-1308 CE) is credited with building the core of the Meenakshi Temple complex, including the main gopurams and mandapams.

questions.question: The Chola-Pandya rivalry often involved the intervention of which other major power, particularly during the 9th and 10th centuries?
questions.explanation: The Rashtrakutas frequently intervened in the Chola-Pandya conflict, often allying with the Pandyas against the rising Chola power, as seen in the battle of Takkolam.

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly differentiates the 'Kudavolai' system of the Cholas from the Pandya administrative practices?
options: It was a method of tax collection unique to the Pandya kingdom
It was a lottery-based system for electing village committee members used by the Cholas
It was a military recruitment system used by both empires
It was a land survey method introduced by the Pandyas
questions.explanation: The Kudavolai system, described in the Uttaramerur inscriptions, was a unique Chola practice of drawing lots to select members for various village committees, not a Pandya practice.

questions.question: The 'Thirumalai Inscription' of the Pandya king Maravarman Sundara Pandya II is historically crucial for its reference to which event?
options: The establishment of the Madurai Sultanate
The construction of the Vaigai dam
The defeat of the Chera king
questions.explanation: The Thirumalai inscription (1311 CE) records the payment of tribute by the Pandya king to Malik Kafur, marking the first major Delhi Sultanate incursion into the deep South.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with the conquest of the Pandya kingdom and the assumption of the title 'Maduraikonda'?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I defeated the Pandya king and captured Madurai, earning the title 'Maduraikonda' (taker of Madurai).

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the 'Velvikudi Grant' as having performed the 'Hiranyagarbha' and 'Tulabhara' rituals was:
questions.explanation: The Velvikudi Grant of the Pandya king Maran Chadayan (also known as Palyagasalai Mudukudumi Peruvaludi) records his performance of these Vedic sacrifices.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler's reign saw the famous 'Kalingattu Parani' being composed, and also witnessed a decisive war against the Pandya confederacy at the Battle of Nettur?
questions.explanation: Kulothunga I's reign featured the Kalinga war (celebrated in Kalingattu Parani) and the Battle of Nettur where he defeated a Pandya-Chera-Sinhala alliance.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is credited with building the 'Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple' in its present form and who was a contemporary of the Vijayanagara ruler Achyuta Deva Raya was:
questions.explanation: Mara Varman Kulasekara Pandya I (13th century) is traditionally credited with the major reconstruction of the Meenakshi Temple, though later expansions occurred under the Nayaks.

questions.question: Which Chola king's 'Uttaramerur Inscriptions' provide detailed information about the village administration system, including the Kudavolai (pot-ticket) system of electing members to the Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Uttaramerur inscriptions of Parantaka I (c. 919-920 CE) detail the constitution of the village assembly and the Kudavolai method of election.

questions.question: The 'Thirumalai' inscription of the Pandya king Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I records his victory over which major contemporary kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Thirumalai inscription (c. 1251 CE) of Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I boasts of his defeat of the Hoysala king Someshwara.

questions.question: Which Chola king is known for his naval expedition to Kadaram (Malay Peninsula) and also for defeating the Pandya king Amarabhujanga?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I's famous naval campaign conquered Kadaram, and his early reign involved subjugating the Pandya king Amarabhujanga.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the 'Maduraikanchi' as a patron of poets and who is often identified with the legendary 'Nedunjeliyan' who killed the Chola king at the Battle of Talaiyalanganam was:
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan II (also known as Aryappadaikadantha Nedunjeliyan) is the Pandya victor of the Battle of Talaiyalanganam, celebrated in the Maduraikanchi.

questions.question: Which Chola king's reign marked the beginning of the 'Later Chola' period and involved a war with the Pandya king Rajasimha II, who was eventually defeated and fled to Ceylon?
questions.explanation: Aditya I (c. 871-907 CE) defeated the Pandya king Rajasimha II at the Battle of Sripurambiyam, forcing him to flee to Ceylon, marking the rise of the Imperial Cholas.

questions.question: The 'Sinnamanur Plates' of the Pandya king Rajasimha II mention the performance of which major Vedic sacrifice, indicating the Pandya claim to imperial status?
questions.explanation: The Sinnamanur Plates of Rajasimha II (c. 920 CE) record the performance of the Ashvamedha (horse sacrifice), a ritual of paramount sovereignty.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with the successful naval expedition against the Srivijaya Empire, and also assumed the title 'Mummudi Chola'?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I led the naval campaign against Srivijaya and used the title Mummudi Chola, meaning 'three-crowned Chola'.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the 'Mandagappattu' inscription as having defeated the Chola king at the Battle of Tellaru is:
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I defeated the Chola king at Tellaru, as recorded in the Mandagappattu inscription.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler is associated with the construction of the 'Gangaikonda Cholapuram' temple and the performance of the 'Hiranyagarbha' and 'Tulabhara' ceremonies?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I built Gangaikonda Cholapuram and performed these Vedic rituals to mark his northern conquests.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is credited with patronizing the Tamil Sangam and is traditionally associated with the 'Third Sangam' at Madurai is:
questions.explanation: Ariyapadaikadantha Nedunjeliyan ('he who defeated the Aryan army') is traditionally linked to the Third Sangam.

questions.question: Which Chola king's reign saw the compilation of the 'Takkayagapparani' by Kalingattuparani, and who also fought the Battle of Koppam?
questions.explanation: Rajadhiraja I fought the Battle of Koppam against the Chalukyas and his reign saw the composition of Kalingattuparani.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is known for his 'Vatteluttu' inscriptions and for building the 'Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple' complex in its early form is:
questions.explanation: Maravarman Kulasekara Pandya I is credited with early construction of the Meenakshi temple and issued Vatteluttu inscriptions.

questions.question: Which Chola king is known for his 'Meykirthi' (praise) inscription that begins with 'Pumalai Pugazhum...' and who defeated the Pandya king Maravarman Sundara Pandya?
questions.explanation: Kulothunga I's meykirthi starts with 'Pumalai Pugazhum' and he defeated Maravarman Sundara Pandya.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is mentioned in the 'Velvikudi Grant' as having performed the 'Hiranyagarbha' and 'Tulabhara' ceremonies, and who revived Pandya power after the Kalabhra interregnum is:
questions.explanation: Kadungon is credited in the Velvikudi grant with ending Kalabhra rule and performing these Vedic rituals.

questions.question: Which Chola king's reign is marked by the 'Uttaramerur' inscriptions that detail the system of 'Kudavolai' (pot-ticket) election for village committees?
questions.explanation: Parantaka I's Uttaramerur inscriptions describe the Kudavolai system of local self-government.

questions.question: The Pandya king who is known for his 'Maduraikkanchi' poem and who is described as the 'Lord of the Southern Ocean' and a patron of the Sangam poet Mangudi Marudanar is:
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan I is praised in Maduraikkanchi by Mangudi Marudanar and called 'Lord of the Southern Ocean'.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler is credited with the systematic introduction of the 'Valanadu' administrative division?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I reorganized the Chola empire into mandalams, valanadus, and nadus, with valanadus being districts under royal officers.

questions.question: The Tamil Sangam period is traditionally associated with which Pandya capital?
questions.explanation: Madurai was the Pandya capital and the legendary seat of the three Tamil Sangams, where literary works were compiled.

questions.question: The famous Nataraja bronze sculptures of the Chola period were created using which metalcasting technique?
questions.explanation: Chola bronzes were crafted using the cire perdue (lost-wax) technique, allowing exquisite detailing in poses like the Nataraja.

questions.question: Which Roman emperor is mentioned in the 'Periplus of the Erythraean Sea' as having received embassies from the Pandya kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Periplus notes that embassies from the Pandya king were sent to the court of Augustus, highlighting early Indo-Roman trade.

questions.question: The Chola naval expedition to Kadaram (Srivijaya) was launched during the reign of which ruler?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I led a successful naval campaign against the Srivijaya empire (Kadaram) in the 11th century, asserting Chola maritime power.

questions.question: Which Sangam poem provides a detailed description of the Pandya port of Korkai and its trade?
questions.explanation: Pattinappalai, a Sangam poem by Kadiyalur Uruttirangannanar, vividly describes the bustling port of Korkai and its pearl trade.

questions.question: The Uttaramerur inscriptions from the Chola period primarily provide information on:
questions.explanation: The Uttaramerur inscriptions detail the functioning of the village sabha, including rules for electing members and managing local affairs.

questions.question: Which Pandya ruler allied with the Rashtrakuta king Krishna III in the Battle of Takkolam against the Cholas?
questions.explanation: Varaguna II of the Pandya dynasty sided with the Rashtrakutas, but the alliance was defeated by the Chola king Parantaka I at Takkolam.

questions.question: The Brihadeeswarar Temple at Thanjavur, built by Rajaraja I, is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva as Brihadeeswara, and its towering vimana is a masterpiece of Chola architecture.

questions.question: According to Tamil tradition, the end of the third Sangam period is attributed to:
questions.explanation: The third Sangam is believed to have ended with the Kalabhra interregnum, which plunged Tamilakam into a dark age before the rise of the Pandyas.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Chola administration is/are correct? 1. The Chola kingdom was divided into Mandalams, Valanadus, and Nadus. 2. The 'Ur' assembly was found in brahmin settlements. 3. The 'Kudavolai' system was used to elect members of the 'Sabha'.
questions.explanation: Ur was for non-brahmin villages, not brahmin; Sabha for brahmin villages used Kudavolai. So statements 1 and 3 are correct.

questions.question: Which Chola king is known for leading a naval expedition to Kadaram (Srivijaya) and assuming the title 'Srivijayendra'?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I launched the successful Kadaram expedition in 1025 CE and took the title Srivijayendra.

questions.question: Which Pandya king is celebrated in the Sangam poem 'Pattinappalai' for his victory over the Chera and Chola at the Battle of Talaiyalanganam?
questions.explanation: Nedunjeliyan I, a Pandya king, is praised in 'Pattinappalai' for his decisive victory at Talaiyalanganam.

questions.question: Which Chola king is known for patronizing the Tamil poet Kamban, who composed the 'Kamba Ramayanam'?
questions.explanation: Kulothunga Chola I (1070-1122 CE) was a patron of Kamban, who authored the Tamil Ramayana.

questions.question: The Uttaramerur inscription, which provides detailed information on the Chola village assembly system, belongs to the reign of which king?
questions.explanation: The Uttaramerur inscription dates to the reign of Parantaka I (907-955 CE) and describes the Kudavolai electoral system.

questions.question: Which Chola king is credited with constructing the Brihadeeswarar Temple at Gangaikonda Cholapuram?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I built the temple at Gangaikonda Cholapuram to commemorate his northern conquests.

questions.question: The ancient Pandya port known for its pearl fisheries was:
questions.explanation: Korkai, near the present-day Tirunelveli, was the center of the Pandya pearl trade.

questions.question: Which of the following was NOT a division of the Chola administrative system?
questions.explanation: Kottam was a territorial division under the Pallavas, not the Cholas; Cholas used Mandalam, Valanadu, Nadu, and Kurram.

questions.question: Which major achievement is attributed to the Pandya king Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I?
options: Conquest of the Chola capital Gangaikonda Cholapuram
Construction of the Meenakshi Temple at Madurai
Defeat of the Chera king at Karur
Establishment of a trading post in Sri Lanka
questions.explanation: Sundara Pandya I (1251-1268) conquered Gangaikonda Cholapuram and subjugated the Chola kingdom.

questions.question: Which is considered the earliest known Pandya inscription, dating to around 2nd-1st century BCE?
questions.explanation: The Mangulam inscription, found near Madurai, records a donation by a Pandya king and is the earliest Pandya epigraph.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Chola-Pandya rivalry: 1) The battle of Takkolam (c. 949 CE) resulted in the defeat of the Chola king Parantaka I by the Rashtrakuta king Krishna III, who later crowned a Pandya prince as the ruler of Madurai. 2) The Pandya king Maravarman Kulasekhara I (c. 1268–1308 CE) successfully repelled the invasion of the Delhi Sultanate under Malik Kafur. 3) The Later Pandyas under Jatavarman Sundara Pandyan I expanded their territory up to Nellore and Kanchi by defeating the Cholas and the Kakatiyas. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Parantaka I was defeated at Takkolam by Krishna III, who installed a Pandya. Statement 2 is false: Maravarman Kulasekhara I faced Malik Kafur's invasion and was defeated. Statement 3 is correct: Jatavarman Sundara Pandyan I defeated the Cholas and Kakatiyas, expanding northward.
